annotations.label: Uniform continuity (informal)

anchor.snippet: is said to be uniformly continuous if there is a positive real number

annotations.note: Mathlib's `UniformContinuous` is the uniform-space generalization of this metric notion.

annotations.label: Uniform continuity on metric spaces

anchor.snippet: is called uniformly continuous if for every real number

annotations.note: The ε–δ characterization of uniform continuity for pseudometric spaces.

annotations.label: Equivalent definition via quantifiers

annotations.note: Same ε–δ statement as the metric definition.

anchor.snippet: is uniformly continuous if it admits a modulus of continuity

annotations.note: Searches found no `modulus of continuity` definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniformly continuous implies continuous

anchor.snippet: Every uniformly continuous function is continuous

annotations.label: Absolutely continuous implies uniformly continuous

anchor.snippet: Any absolutely continuous function

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a function-level `AbsolutelyContinuous` predicate yielding this implication.

annotations.label: Image of totally bounded set

anchor.snippet: The image of a totally bounded subset under a uniformly continuous function is totally bounded

annotations.note: Stated exactly at line 663 of Cauchy.lean.

annotations.label: Heine–Cantor theorem

anchor.snippet: The Heine–Cantor theorem asserts that every continuous function on a compact set is uniformly continuous

annotations.note: Heine-Cantor theorem in the uniform-space setting.

annotations.label: Continuous function with finite limit at infinity is uniformly continuous

anchor.snippet: is continuous on

annotations.note: Mathlib generalizes `limit at infinity` to convergence along the cocompact filter.

annotations.label: Linear functions

anchor.snippet: Linear functions

annotations.note: Continuous linear maps between uniform additive groups are uniformly continuous; the elementary real-line example is implicit.

annotations.label: Continuous on compact interval

anchor.snippet: Any continuous function on the interval

annotations.note: Special case of Heine-Cantor applied to a compact subset like [a,b].

annotations.label: Bounded derivative implies uniform continuity

anchor.snippet: If a function is differentiable on an open interval and its derivative is bounded

annotations.note: Available by composing `lipschitzWith_of_nnnorm_deriv_le` with `LipschitzWith.uniformContinuous`; no single combined decl.

annotations.label: Lipschitz and Hölder are uniformly continuous

anchor.snippet: Every Lipschitz continuous map between two metric spaces is uniformly continuous

annotations.note: Lipschitz case verified at line 188; Hölder case is `HolderWith.uniformContinuous` in Mathlib/Topology/MetricSpace/Holder.lean.

annotations.label: Absolute value function

anchor.snippet: The absolute value function is uniformly continuous

annotations.note: Verified at line 61 of Real/Lemmas.lean.

annotations.label: Weierstrass function

anchor.snippet: Despite being nowhere differentiable, the Weierstrass function is uniformly continuous

annotations.note: `uniformContinuous_weierstrass` exists in Counterexamples/NowhereDifferentiable.lean but is outside the Mathlib library.

annotations.label: Uniformly equicontinuous members

anchor.snippet: Every member of a uniformly equicontinuous set of functions is uniformly continuous

annotations.note: Verified at line 372 of Equicontinuity.lean.

annotations.label: Tangent function nonexample

anchor.snippet: The tangent function is continuous on the interval

annotations.note: No declaration asserts non-uniform-continuity of `Real.tan` on `(-π/2, π/2)`.

annotations.label: Exponential function nonexample

anchor.snippet: The exponential function

annotations.note: No declaration in Mathlib asserts that `Real.exp` is not uniformly continuous on ℝ.

annotations.note: No microcontinuity notion appears in Mathlib (though hyperreals exist in `Mathlib.Analysis.Real.Hyperreal`).

annotations.label: Uniformly continuous implies Cauchy-continuous

anchor.snippet: every uniformly continuous function is Cauchy-continuous

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes Cauchy-continuity as preservation of Cauchy sequences.

annotations.label: Linear maps: continuous iff uniformly continuous

annotations.label: Generalized Heine–Cantor theorem
